bank.title: Bank Consumer Complaint
bank.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against banks in {city} for fraud, mis-selling, or poor service. Get ₹199 Lawly legal notice in minutes.
bank.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Banking Regulation Act 1949
key_rights: Right to full disclosure of all loan charges and processing fees
Right to compensation for unauthorised transactions within 48 hours
Right to reverse mis-sold insurance products bundled with loans
Right to receive NPA notice 60 days before account classification
common_complaints: Mis-sold credit card insurance or ULIPs
steps: Send a legal notice to the bank's Nodal Officer
File complaint on RBI Sachet portal (sachet.rbi.org.in)
Approach Banking Ombudsman if no resolution in 30 days
File at DCDRC in {city} for compensation
bank.time_limit: 2 years from date of deficiency of service
bank.compensation: Up to ₹1 crore + legal costs + mental harassment

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against a private bank in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Both public and private banks are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. You can file at the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (DCDRC) in {city}.

faqs.q: How long does a bank consumer complaint take in {city}?
faqs.a: DCDRC is mandated to resolve within 90 days. Banking Ombudsman takes 30–60 days. A legal notice often triggers settlement within 15 days.

faqs.q: Is a lawyer required to file a consumer complaint against a bank in {city}?
faqs.a: No. You can self-represent at DCDRC. Lawly's AI-drafted legal notice (₹199) is the fastest first step.

insurance.title: Insurance Consumer Complaint
insurance.meta_desc: Send a legal notice to LIC, Star Health, or any insurer in {city} for claim rejection or delay. Lawly from ₹199.
insurance.regulator: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI)
key_rights: Right to claim settlement within 30 days of receiving final survey report
Right to 2% interest per month on delayed claim settlement
Right to written reasons for any claim rejection
Right to free-look cancellation within 30 days of policy receipt
common_complaints: Health claim rejected for 'pre-existing condition' without disclosure
steps: Send a legal notice to the insurer's Grievance Officer
File complaint on IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal
Approach Insurance Ombudsman (free, up to ₹50 lakh)
File at DCDRC in {city} for higher amounts or compensation
insurance.time_limit: 2 years from date of claim rejection
insurance.compensation: Claim amount + 2% monthly interest + ₹25,000–₹2 lakh mental agony

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against LIC in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. LIC is covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. You can file at the DCDRC in {city} or approach the Insurance Ombudsman free of charge for claims up to ₹50 lakh.

faqs.q: What can I get if my health insurance claim is wrongly rejected?
faqs.a: You can claim the full claim amount + 2% monthly interest on delay + compensation for mental agony (typically ₹25,000–₹2 lakh) + litigation costs at DCDRC {city}.

faqs.q: How quickly does a legal notice resolve an insurance dispute?
faqs.a: In most cases, sending a properly drafted legal notice to the insurer's Grievance Officer resolves the matter in 15–30 days before any forum is approached.

ecommerce.title: E-Commerce Consumer Complaint
ecommerce.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against Amazon, Flipkart, Meesho in {city} for refund delays or fake products. Lawly from ₹199.
key_rights: Right to refund within the platform's stated refund policy (enforceable)
Right to compensation for counterfeit or defective goods
Right to full product disclosure before purchase
Right to safe delivery and compensation for damage in transit
common_complaints: Item not delivered but marked as delivered
Wrong item delivered and return rejected
steps: Send a legal notice to the platform's Grievance Officer
File on National Consumer Helpline (1915 / consumerhelpline.gov.in)
File at DCDRC in {city} for unresolved complaints
Claim compensation for mental agony and loss
ecommerce.time_limit: 2 years from date of purchase / non-delivery
ecommerce.compensation: Full refund + replacement value + ₹10,000–₹1 lakh for harassment

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against Amazon India in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Amazon India (Amazon Seller Services Pvt. Ltd.) is covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019 and E-Commerce Rules 2020. You can file at DCDRC in {city}.

faqs.q: What if the e-commerce platform refuses my refund?
faqs.a: Send a legal notice to Amazon/Flipkart's Grievance Officer (available on their website). If no response in 30 days, file at DCDRC {city}. Courts consistently award refunds + compensation.

faqs.q: Does DCDRC in {city} handle online shopping complaints?
faqs.a: Yes. Under CPA 2019, you can file in the city where you live (your residential address), not where the company is registered.

telecom.title: Telecom Consumer Complaint
telecom.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against Airtel, Jio, Vi in {city} for call drops, billing errors, or service issues. Lawly from ₹199.
telecom.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, TRAI Act 1997, Telecom Consumer Protection Regulations 2012
key_rights: Right to 7-day free trial for new connections before commitment
Right to pro-rated refund on prepaid balance under TRAI rules
Right to free port-out (MNP) without any cancellation charges
Right to service credits for persistent call drops or outages
common_complaints: Broadband speed far below committed speed
steps: Send a legal notice to the telecom operator's Nodal Officer
File complaint on TRAI / DoT grievance portal
Approach TDSAT if TRAI resolution unsatisfactory
File at DCDRC in {city} for compensation
telecom.time_limit: 2 years from date of service deficiency

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against Airtel or Jio in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Telecom companies are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. DCDRC in {city} has jurisdiction because you receive the service at your {city} address.

faqs.q: What if my internet speed is much lower than promised?
faqs.a: Use TRAI MySpeed app to record speed tests. Send a legal notice citing TRAI's Quality of Service (QoS) regulations. Courts in {city} have awarded refunds and compensation for persistent slow speeds.

faqs.q: Can I get compensation for call drops?
faqs.a: Yes. TRAI QoS regulations mandate minimum standards. Persistent call drops constitute a deficiency of service. File at DCDRC {city} for monthly plan refund + mental agony compensation.

airline.title: Airline Consumer Complaint
airline.meta_desc: Send a legal notice for flight cancellation, delay, or refund denial against IndiGo, Air India, SpiceJet in {city}. Lawly from ₹199.
airline.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, DGCA Civil Aviation Requirements (CAR), Montreal Convention
key_rights: Right to full refund for airline-cancelled flights within 7 days
Right to meal vouchers for delays over 2 hours (DGCA CAR)
Right to compensation of ₹5,000–₹10,000 for denied boarding
Right to ₹3,500–₹7,500 for baggage loss/damage (domestic flights)
common_complaints: Flight cancelled without adequate notice or refund
Long delay without meals or hotel accommodation
steps: Send a legal notice to the airline's Grievance Officer
File complaint on DGCA Airline Complaints portal
Approach National Consumer Helpline (1915)
File at DCDRC in {city} for full compensation
airline.time_limit: 2 years from date of flight / incident
airline.compensation: Ticket refund + DGCA mandated amounts + ₹25,000–₹1 lakh harassment

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against IndiGo or Air India in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Airlines are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. You can file at DCDRC in {city} since the service was to be received at your departure/arrival city.

faqs.q: My flight was cancelled and I got no refund. What can I claim?
faqs.a: Full ticket refund + DGCA-mandated compensation (₹5,000–₹10,000) + compensation for hotel/rebooking costs + mental agony at DCDRC {city}.

faqs.q: How fast can I get money back from an airline in {city}?
faqs.a: A Lawly legal notice (₹199) typically prompts airlines to process the refund within 15 days. If ignored, DCDRC {city} orders refund + interest + compensation.

realestate.title: Real Estate / Builder Consumer Complaint
realestate.meta_desc: File a RERA complaint or consumer complaint against builders in {city} for delayed possession or wrong promises. Lawly from ₹199.
key_rights: Right to 5% interest per annum on deposited amount for delayed possession
Right to refund with interest if builder fails to deliver
Right to RERA-registered project details and approvals
Right to no unilateral changes to approved plans
common_complaints: Builder demanding extra charges not in agreement
Construction quality defects (structural defects for 5 years)
steps: Send a legal notice to the builder's Managing Director
File complaint with State RERA Authority in {state}
Approach DCDRC in {city} if RERA not applicable or additional compensation needed
Claim interest + compensation + refund
realestate.time_limit: 3 years under RERA; 2 years at DCDRC
realestate.compensation: Full refund with 5% interest + ₹50,000–₹5 lakh mental agony

faqs.q: Should I approach RERA or consumer court in {city} for a builder complaint?
faqs.a: RERA is faster for possession delays and financial penalties. DCDRC in {city} also has jurisdiction and can award larger mental agony compensation. Many homebuyers file in both.

faqs.q: Can I get my investment back if the builder defaults in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Under RERA, you can get full refund + 5% interest per annum. DCDRC {city} can additionally award compensation for mental harassment and loss of rental income.

faqs.q: Is a legal notice effective against builders in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Builders often settle after a properly drafted legal notice to avoid RERA scrutiny. Lawly's ₹199 notice is the fastest and cheapest first step before filing a formal complaint.

automobile.title: Automobile Consumer Complaint
automobile.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against Maruti, Hyundai, Tata Motors in {city} for manufacturing defects or service issues. Lawly from ₹199.
automobile.regulator: National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission + DCDRC
key_rights: Right to replacement or full refund for a vehicle with manufacturing defect
Right to free repairs during warranty period
Right to proper disclosure of recall notices
Right to product liability compensation under CPA 2019 §83
common_complaints: Manufacturing defect not acknowledged by dealer
Warranty claim rejected for minor reasons
Vehicle not fixed after multiple service visits
steps: Send a legal notice to the dealer + manufacturer's Customer Care Head
File complaint on National Consumer Helpline (1915)
Get expert report documenting the manufacturing defect
File at DCDRC in {city} for replacement / refund
automobile.time_limit: 2 years from date of defect discovery
automobile.compensation: Full vehicle cost refund OR replacement + ₹50,000–₹3 lakh harassment

faqs.q: Can I get my car replaced if it has a manufacturing defect in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Under CPA 2019 §83 (product liability), you can claim a full replacement or refund. DCDRC {city} has repeatedly ordered car replacements against Maruti, Hyundai, and Tata Motors.

faqs.q: My warranty was rejected on a technicality. Can I fight this in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Courts in {city} have consistently held that warranty terms cannot be used to defeat legitimate defect claims. A Lawly legal notice creates a formal record before you file.

faqs.q: Do I need an expert report to file an automobile complaint?
faqs.a: Not for filing — but it strengthens your case significantly. DCDRC {city} can appoint a court commissioner to inspect the vehicle if you request it.

medical.title: Medical Negligence Consumer Complaint
medical.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint for medical negligence against hospitals in {city}. Get Lawly legal notice from ₹199.
medical.regulator: National Medical Commission (NMC) + State Medical Council
medical.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019; IMA v. VP Shantha (1995 SC); NMC Act 2020
medical.governing_law_short: CPA 2019 + IMA v. VP Shantha (1995 SC)
medical.regulator_name: National Medical Commission / State Medical Council
key_rights: Right to receive full treatment details and medical records within 72 hours
Right to prior informed consent before any procedure
Right to compensation for negligence under both CPA and tort law
Right to second opinion without denial of primary treatment
common_complaints: Wrong diagnosis leading to unnecessary surgery
steps: Obtain all medical records immediately (request formally in writing)
Send legal notice to the hospital's Medical Superintendent
File complaint with State Medical Council for license action
File at DCDRC in {city} for compensation
medical.time_limit: 2 years from date of negligent act or discovery
medical.compensation: Treatment costs + income loss + ₹2 lakh–₹5 crore depending on severity

faqs.q: Are doctors and private hospitals covered under consumer law in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Since IMA v. VP Shantha (1995 SC), medical services are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. DCDRC {city} has jurisdiction to award compensation against any private hospital.

faqs.q: What proof do I need for a medical negligence complaint in {city}?
faqs.a: Hospital medical records, discharge summary, prescriptions, and ideally an independent medical expert opinion. DCDRC {city} can also appoint a medical expert as court commissioner.

faqs.q: Can I file alone or do I need a lawyer for medical negligence in {city}?
faqs.a: You can self-represent at DCDRC {city}. However, for higher-value claims (above ₹20 lakh), engaging a consumer lawyer in {city} is advisable. Lawly's legal notice (₹199) is the best first step.

courier.title: Courier / Logistics Consumer Complaint
courier.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against Blue Dart, DTDC, Delhivery in {city} for lost or damaged shipment. Lawly from ₹199.
courier.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Indian Contract Act 1872
key_rights: Right to compensation for full declared value of lost shipment
Right to reject liability-cap clauses that violate CPA 2019
Right to compensation for consequential losses from courier delay
Right to documented proof of delivery or acknowledgement of non-delivery
common_complaints: Shipment marked delivered but not received
Courier refusing to pay beyond artificial liability cap
steps: Send a legal notice to the courier company's Grievance Officer
File complaint on National Consumer Helpline (1915)
File at DCDRC in {city} for full declared value + damages
Challenge artificial liability-cap clauses as unfair trade practice
courier.time_limit: 2 years from date of non-delivery or damage
courier.compensation: Full shipment value + consequential losses + ₹5,000–₹50,000 for harassment

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against Blue Dart or DTDC in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Courier services are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. You can file at DCDRC in {city} since you are the sender/receiver in {city}.

faqs.q: The courier says their liability is capped at ₹500. Is that valid?
faqs.a: No. Consumer courts in {city} and across India have consistently held that artificial liability caps in courier T&C are an unfair trade practice under CPA 2019 §2(47). You can claim full value.

faqs.q: My package was marked delivered but I never received it. What can I do?
faqs.a: File a police complaint (FIR) and simultaneously send a Lawly legal notice (₹199) to the courier demanding GPS records and proof of delivery. Then file at DCDRC {city}.

food.title: Food Delivery Consumer Complaint
food.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against Zomato or Swiggy in {city} for food quality or delivery issues. Lawly from ₹199.
food.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Food Safety and Standards Act 2006
key_rights: Right to food that meets FSSAI safety standards
Right to accurate ingredient and allergen disclosure
Right to full refund for spoiled or contaminated food
Right to compensation for food poisoning causing health harm
common_complaints: Food delivered cold, spoiled, or wrong order
steps: Photograph and preserve evidence of defective food
Send a legal notice to the platform's Grievance Officer
File complaint with FSSAI if food safety standards breached
File at DCDRC in {city} for compensation
food.compensation: Full refund + medical expenses + ₹10,000–₹1 lakh mental agony

faqs.q: Can I file a consumer complaint against Zomato or Swiggy in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Zomato India Pvt. Ltd. and Bundl Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (Swiggy) are covered under CPA 2019. DCDRC {city} has jurisdiction since you ordered from {city}.

faqs.q: I got food poisoning from a Swiggy order. What can I claim?
faqs.a: Medical treatment costs + 3× the order value as minimum compensation + mental agony damages at DCDRC {city}. Preserve doctor prescriptions and the food packaging.

faqs.q: Zomato denied my refund for a wrong order. What next?
faqs.a: Send a Lawly legal notice (₹199) to Zomato's Grievance Officer. If unresolved in 15 days, file at DCDRC {city}. Courts have consistently ordered refunds + compensation of ₹5,000–₹15,000.

education.title: Education / College Fee Refund Consumer Complaint
education.meta_desc: Get coaching fee refund or file a complaint against your college or coaching centre in {city}. Lawly from ₹199.
education.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, UGC Fee Refund Policy 2023
education.regulator_name: UGC Grievance Portal / National Consumer Helpline
key_rights: Right to full fee refund (minus processing) if you cancel before 15 days of class start (UGC 2023)
Right to proportionate refund under UGC policy even mid-term for genuine medical reasons
Right to receive promised faculty, infrastructure, and course materials
Right to fee receipt and written agreement for coaching services
common_complaints: Coaching centre refusing fee refund on cancellation
Promised faculty / online content not delivered
College deducting more than UGC permits on withdrawal
Diploma / certification not issued after course completion
Online course platform subscription not refunded after technical failure
steps: Send a legal notice to the institution's Director / CEO
File complaint on UGC Grievance Portal (grievance.ugc.ac.in)
File at DCDRC in {city} citing UGC fee refund policy
Claim full fee + mental agony compensation
education.time_limit: 2 years from date of fee payment / breach
education.compensation: Full fee refund + ₹10,000–₹1 lakh mental agony

faqs.q: Can I get a refund from a coaching centre in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Coaching centres are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. UGC's fee refund policy (updated 2023) provides a tiered refund schedule based on timing of withdrawal.

faqs.q: My college in {city} won't refund my fees even though I withdrew. What can I do?
faqs.a: Send a legal notice citing UGC Circular F.No.1-3/2022(CPP-II). If the college doesn't respond within 15 days, file at DCDRC {city}. Courts have ordered full fee refunds + compensation.

faqs.q: BYJU'S sold me an expensive course that doesn't work. Can I get a refund in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. DCDRC courts across India have ordered BYJU'S to refund fees due to mis-selling and non-delivery of promised content. A Lawly legal notice is your fastest first step.

electricity.title: Electricity Board Consumer Complaint
electricity.meta_desc: File a consumer complaint against your electricity board in {city} for overbilling or wrong meter reading. Lawly from ₹199.
key_rights: Right to accurate billing based on meter reading
Right to meter testing at your request (Electricity Act §55)
Right to compensation for power outages beyond 4 hours (SERC norms)
Right to new connection within 30 days of application
common_complaints: New connection application delayed beyond 30 days
steps: Send a legal notice to the local DISCOM's Grievance Officer
File complaint with State Electricity Regulatory Commission
Approach Electricity Ombudsman (free)
File at DCDRC in {city} for compensation
electricity.time_limit: 2 years from date of defective billing / service failure

faqs.q: Can I challenge my electricity bill at a consumer court in {city}?
faqs.a: Yes. Electricity distribution companies are covered under the Consumer Protection Act 2019. DCDRC {city} has jurisdiction alongside the Electricity Ombudsman.

faqs.q: My electricity meter is wrong. How do I prove it?
faqs.a: Under Section 55 of the Electricity Act, you can formally request meter testing. If the meter is found defective, the board must adjust all bills for the preceding 6 months.

faqs.q: The electricity board is not responding to my complaint in {city}. What next?
faqs.a: File a Lawly legal notice (₹199) to the DISCOM's Grievance Officer, then escalate to the State Electricity Ombudsman. If still unresolved, DCDRC {city} is the fastest option.
